summary: Time-Series AI Learning Progress bug fix verified. The fix correctly reads model.metrics.accuracy instead of test_accuracy and checks model.trained flag. All backend tests passed (7/7), frontend displays correct values after data load.

details: Endpoint responds with correct structure for frontend consumption
Training returns metrics with accuracy field
Overall progress shows 75% (increased from ~38% before fix)
AI Model Training shows 100% with 'Model trained (50.4% accuracy)'
AI Model Training shows 'Ready' badge when trained
Prediction Tracking shows 100% with '64651 predictions verified'
Strategy Simulations shows 100% with '10 backtests completed'
Card displays correctly after data refresh
Train All button present with data-testid='train-all-btn'

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: Time-Series AI model training was not reflected in NIA Learning Progress tracker after clicking 'Train All'
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: Frontend was looking for metrics.test_accuracy but backend returns metrics.accuracy. Also missing check for model.trained flag.
fix_applied: modelTrained now correctly checks timeseriesTrained OR timeseriesAccuracy !== null (Line 2293)
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: CONFIRMED - Learning Progress now shows 75% overall instead of 38%

seed_data_creation: N/A - Used existing trained model data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Time-Series AI Learning Progress bug fix verified. Key data-testids: nav-nia, train-all-btn. The model is trained with 64,651 samples and 50.4% accuracy. Learning Progress shows 75% overall. Note: Initial page load may show 0% momentarily before data loads - clicking 'Refresh Intel' ensures data is displayed.

rca_of_issue.bug_description: Time-Series AI model training was not being reflected in the NIA Learning Progress tracker after clicking 'Train All'
reproduction_steps: 3. Observe that 'AI Model Training' still shows 0% or no progress
rca_of_issue.root_cause: Frontend code in NIA.jsx was looking for wrong metric field. Line 2249 originally checked for ts?.model?.metrics?.test_accuracy but the backend API returns metrics.accuracy (not test_accuracy). Additionally, the model.trained flag was not being checked.
fix_details: Line 2249: Changed from test_accuracy to accuracy
Line 2251: Added check for model.trained flag
Line 2293: modelTrained now checks BOTH timeseriesTrained OR timeseriesAccuracy
rca_of_issue.verification: After fix, Learning Progress shows 75% overall (was ~38%), AI Model Training shows 100% with accuracy percentage displayed
